title: Fresh Gooey Korean Olive Dumplings
description: A midnightcraving dumpling dish with juicy, pitted black olives and a silky, savory filling. These kosherfriendly dumplings are crisp on the outside and incredibly gooey on the inside.
instructions: Combine ground chicken, chopped olives, garlic, ginger, soy sauce, sesame oil, egg, salt, pepper, and water in a bowl; mix until well incorporated and slightly sticky.
Place a handful of the filling onto the center of each dumpling wrapper; fold the wrapper over and pinch the edges to seal, forming half‑moon shapes.
Heat vegetable oil in a large non‑stick skillet over medium heat.
Add the dumplings in a single layer, making sure they don't touch; cook for 2–3 minutes until the bottoms are golden brown.
Pour in about 1/4 cup of water, cover the skillet with a lid, and let the dumplings steam for 4–5 minutes until the water has evaporated and the filling is cooked through (internal temp 165°F).
Remove the lid and continue cooking for an additional 1–2 minutes to crisp the bottoms slightly.
Transfer cooked dumplings to a plate and garnish with sliced scallions.
Serve hot with a side of chili oil or a simple dipping sauce. Enjoy the gooey, olive‑filled center!
